briefdescription.para: Packets may be framed or unframed.
Packets are always framed.
QoS Framing.
LE 1M PHY.
LE 2M PHY.
LE Coded PHY.
QoS Preferred PHY.
The periodic advertising has not been synchronized.
Waiting for SyncInfo from Broadcast Assistant.
Synchronized to periodic advertising.
Failed to synchronized to periodic advertising.
No periodic advertising sync transfer receiver from Broadcast Assistant.
Periodic advertising state reported by the Scan Delegator.
The Broadcast Isochronous Group not encrypted.
The Broadcast Isochronous Group broadcast code requested.
The Broadcast Isochronous Group decrypted.
The Broadcast Isochronous Group bad broadcast code.
Broadcast Isochronous Group encryption state reported by the Scan Delegator.
Opcode not supported.
Invalid source ID supplied.
Broadcast Audio Scan Service (BASS) specific ATT error codes.
Audio Stream Endpoint Idle state.
Audio Stream Endpoint Codec Configured state.
Audio Stream Endpoint QoS Configured state.
Audio Stream Endpoint Enabling state.
Audio Stream Endpoint Streaming state.
Audio Stream Endpoint Disabling state.
Audio Stream Endpoint Streaming state.
Endpoint states.
Server completed operation successfully.
Server did not support operation by client.
Server rejected due to invalid operation length.
Invalid ASE ID.
Invalid ASE state.
Invalid operation for direction.
Capabilities not supported by server.
Configuration parameters not supported by server.
Configuration parameters rejected by server.
Invalid Configuration parameters.
Unsupported metadata.
Metadata rejected by server.
Invalid metadata.
Server has insufficient resources.
Unspecified error.
Response Status Code.

detaileddescription.para: These are sent by the server to the client when a stream operation is requested.

briefdescription.para: No reason.
Codec ID.
Codec configuration.
SDU interval.
Framing.
PHY.
Maximum SDU size.
RTN.
Max transport latency.
Presendation delay.
Invalid CIS mapping.
Response Reasons.

detaileddescription.para: These are used if the bt_bap_ascs_rsp_code value is BT_BAP_ASCS_RSP_CODE_CONF_UNSUPPORTED, BT_BAP_ASCS_RSP_CODE_CONF_REJECTED or BT_BAP_ASCS_RSP_CODE_CONF_INVALID.

memberdef.definition: typedef bool(* bt_bap_scan_delegator_state_func_t) (const struct bt_bap_scan_delegator_recv_state *recv_state, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: bt_bap_scan_delegator_state_func_t

briefdescription.para: Callback function for Scan Delegator receive state search functions.

parameterdescription.para: to stop iterating. If this is used in the context of bt_bap_scan_delegator_find_state(), the recv_state will be returned by bt_bap_scan_delegator_find_state()

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* bt_bap_broadcast_assistant_write_cb) (struct bt_conn *conn, int err)
memberdef.name: bt_bap_broadcast_assistant_write_cb

briefdescription.para: Callback function for writes.

parameterdescription.para: The connection to the peer device.
Error value. 0 on success, GATT error on fail.

briefdescription.para: Return structure holding information of audio stream endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: The structure object to be filled with the info.
if ep or info are NULL

briefdescription.para: Get the pointer to the ACL connection of an endpoint.

detaileddescription.para: The caller gets a new reference to the connection object, if not NULL, which must be released with bt_conn_unref() once done using the object.

parameterdescription.para: The endpoint to get the ACL connection of

simplesect.para:
The ACL connection pointer, or NULL if:
ep is NULL
ep is a broadcast endpoint
ep is a Unicast Server endpoint not yet configured by a remote client
ep is a Unicast Client endpoint not yet discovered on a remote server

briefdescription.para: Register Audio callbacks for a stream.

detaileddescription.para: Register Audio callbacks for a stream.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, struct bt_bap_stream *stream, struct bt_bap_ep *ep, struct bt_audio_codec_cfg *codec_cfg)
memberdef.name: bt_bap_stream_config

briefdescription.para: Configure Audio Stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a client to configure a new stream using the remote endpoint, local capability and codec configuration.

simplesect.para: Allocated Audio Stream object or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Reconfigure Audio Stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a unicast client or unicast server to reconfigure a stream to use a different local codec configuration.
This can only be done for unicast streams.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Configure Audio Stream QoS.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a client to configure the Quality of Service of streams in a unicast group. All streams in the group for the specified conn will have the Quality of Service configured. This shall only be used to configure unicast streams.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_bap_stream *stream, const uint8_t meta[], size_t meta_len)
memberdef.name: bt_bap_stream_enable

briefdescription.para: Enable Audio Stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a client to enable a stream.
This shall only be called for unicast streams, as broadcast streams will always be enabled once created.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_bap_stream *stream, const uint8_t meta[], size_t meta_len)
memberdef.name: bt_bap_stream_metadata

briefdescription.para: Change Audio Stream Metadata.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a unicast client or unicast server to change the metadata of a stream.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Disable Audio Stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a unicast client or unicast server to disable a stream.
This shall only be called for unicast streams, as broadcast streams will always be enabled once created.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Connect unicast audio stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a unicast client to connect the connected isochronous stream (CIS) associated with the audio stream. If two audio streams share a CIS, then this only needs to be done once for those streams. This can only be done for streams in the QoS configured or enabled states.
The bt_bap_stream_ops.connected() callback will be called on the streams once this has finished.
This shall only be called for unicast streams, and only as the unicast client (CONFIG_BT_BAP_UNICAST_CLIENT).

parameterdescription.para: if the stream, endpoint, ISO channel or connection is NULL
if the stream or ISO channel is in an invalid state for connection
if the role of the stream is not BT_CONN_ROLE_CENTRAL
if the ISO channel is already connecting or connected
if another ISO channel is connecting
if otherwise rejected by the ISO layer

briefdescription.para: Start Audio Stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a unicast client or unicast server to make a stream start streaming.

For the unicast client, this will send the receiver start ready command to the unicast server for BT_AUDIO_DIR_SOURCE ASEs. The CIS is required to be connected first by bt_bap_stream_connect() before the command can be sent.

For the unicast server, this will execute the receiver start ready command on the unicast server for BT_AUDIO_DIR_SINK ASEs. If the CIS is not connected yet, the stream will go into the streaming state as soon as the CIS is connected.
This shall only be called for unicast streams.
Broadcast sinks will always be started once synchronized, and broadcast source streams shall be started with bt_bap_broadcast_source_start().

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Stop Audio Stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a client to make a stream stop streaming.
This shall only be called for unicast streams. Broadcast sinks cannot be stopped. Broadcast sources shall be stopped with bt_bap_broadcast_source_stop().

parameterdescription.para: The stream does not have an endpoint or a connection, of the stream's connection's role is not BT_CONN_ROLE_CENTRAL
The state of the stream endpoint is not BT_BAP_EP_STATE_DISABLING
The CIS state of the is not in a connected state, and thus is already stopping
The stream is busy with another operation
The stream ACL connection is not connected
The request was rejected by GATT

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Release Audio Stream.

detaileddescription.para: This procedure is used by a unicast client or unicast server to release a unicast stream.
Broadcast sink streams cannot be released, but can be deleted by bt_bap_broadcast_sink_delete(). Broadcast source streams cannot be released, but can be deleted by bt_bap_broadcast_source_delete().

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Send data to Audio stream without timestamp.

detaileddescription.para: Send data from buffer to the stream.

simplesect.para: Support for sending must be supported, determined by CONFIG_BT_AUDIO_TX.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer containing data to be sent.
Packet Sequence number. This value shall be incremented for each call to this function and at least once per SDU interval for a specific channel.

simplesect.para: Bytes sent in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_bap_stream *stream, struct net_buf *buf, uint16_t seq_num, uint32_t ts)
memberdef.name: bt_bap_stream_send_ts

briefdescription.para: Send data to Audio stream with timestamp.

detaileddescription.para: Send data from buffer to the stream.

simplesect.para: Support for sending must be supported, determined by CONFIG_BT_AUDIO_TX.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer containing data to be sent.
Packet Sequence number. This value shall be incremented for each call to this function and at least once per SDU interval for a specific channel.
Timestamp of the SDU in microseconds (us). This value can be used to transmit multiple SDUs in the same SDU interval in a CIG or BIG.

simplesect.para: Bytes sent in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get ISO transmission timing info for a Basic Audio Profile stream.

detaileddescription.para: Reads timing information for transmitted ISO packet on an ISO channel. The HCI_LE_Read_ISO_TX_Sync HCI command is used to retrieve this information from the controller.

simplesect.para: An SDU must have already been successfully transmitted on the ISO channel for this function to return successfully. Support for sending must be supported, determined by CONFIG_BT_AUDIO_TX.

parameterdescription.para: if the stream is invalid, if the stream is not configured for sending or if it is not connected with a isochronous stream

briefdescription.para: Register the Basic Audio Profile Scan Delegator and BASS.

detaileddescription.para: Register the scan deligator and Broadcast Audio Scan Service (BASS) dynamically at runtime.
Only one set of callbacks can be registered at any one time, and calling this function multiple times will override any previously registered callbacks.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: unregister the Basic Audio Profile Scan Delegator and BASS.

detaileddescription.para: Unregister the scan deligator and Broadcast Audio Scan Service (BASS) dynamically at runtime.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Set the periodic advertising sync state to syncing.

detaileddescription.para: Set the periodic advertising sync state for a receive state to syncing, notifying Broadcast Assistants.

parameterdescription.para: The source id used to identify the receive state.
The Periodic Advertising sync state to set.

simplesect.para: int Error value. 0 on success, errno on fail.

briefdescription.para: Set the sync state of a receive state in the server.

parameterdescription.para: The source id used to identify the receive state.
Array of bitfields to set the BIS sync state for each subgroup.

simplesect.para: int Error value. 0 on success, ERRNO on fail.

briefdescription.para: Add a receive state source locally.

detaileddescription.para: This will notify any connected clients about the new source. This allows them to modify and even remove it.
If CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SINK is enabled, any Broadcast Sink sources are autonomously added.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for adding the new source

simplesect.para: int errno on failure, or source ID on success.

briefdescription.para: Add a receive state source locally.

detaileddescription.para: This will notify any connected clients about the new source. This allows them to modify and even remove it.
If CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SINK is enabled, any Broadcast Sink sources are autonomously modified.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for adding the new source

simplesect.para: int errno on failure, or source ID on success.

briefdescription.para: Remove a receive state source.

detaileddescription.para: This will remove the receive state. If the receive state periodic advertising is synced, bt_bap_scan_delegator_cb.pa_sync_term_req() will be called.
If CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SINK is enabled, any Broadcast Sink sources are autonomously removed.

simplesect.para: int Error value. 0 on success, errno on fail.

briefdescription.para: Iterate through all existing receive states.

parameterdescription.para: User specified data that sent to the callback function

briefdescription.para: Find and return a receive state based on a compare function.

parameterdescription.para: User specified data that sent to the callback function

simplesect.para: The first receive state where the func returned true, or NULL

briefdescription.para: Discover Broadcast Audio Scan Service on the server.

detaileddescription.para: Warning: Only one connection can be active at any time; discovering for a new connection, will delete all previous data.

parameterdescription.para: Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Scan start for BISes for a remote server.

detaileddescription.para: This will let the Broadcast Audio Scan Service server know that this device is actively scanning for broadcast sources. The function can optionally also start scanning, if the caller does not want to start scanning itself.
Scan results, if start_scan is true, is sent to the bt_bap_broadcast_assistant_scan_cb callback.

parameterdescription.para: Connection to the Broadcast Audio Scan Service server. Used to let the server know that we are scanning.
Start scanning if true. If false, the application should enable scan itself.
conn is NULL of if conn has not done discovery
Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Stop remote scanning for BISes for a server.

parameterdescription.para: conn is NULL of if conn has not done discovery
Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Registers the callbacks used by Broadcast Audio Scan Service client.
Unregisters the callbacks used by the Broadcast Audio Scan Service client.
Add a source on the server.

parameterdescription.para: conn is NULL or p conn has not done discovery or if param is invalid
Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Modify a source on the server.

parameterdescription.para: conn is NULL or p conn has not done discovery or if param is invalid
Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Set a broadcast code to the specified receive state.

parameterdescription.para: Source ID of the receive state.
conn is NULL or p conn has not done discovery or src_id is invalid
Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Remove a source from the server.

parameterdescription.para: Source ID of the receive state.
conn is NULL or p conn has not done discovery or src_id is invalid
Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Read the specified receive state from the server.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the receive start (0 up to the value from bt_bap_broadcast_assistant_discover_cb)
conn is NULL or p conn has not done discovery or src_id is invalid
Another operation is already in progress for this conn
Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Maximum number of subgroups supported in the BAP Scan Delegator API.
Maximum size of BASE excluding service data header.
An invalid Broadcast ID.
Value that represents an unset presentation delay value.
Recommended connectable advertising parameters.

detaileddescription.para: If connection has not been established after 30 seconds, the device should switch to BT_BAP_ADV_PARAM_CONN_REDUCED
Defined by Table 8.1 in BAP 1.0.2

briefdescription.para: Reduced connectable advertising parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Defined by Table 8.1 in BAP 1.0.2

briefdescription.para: Recommended connection parameters for initial connection request for 7.5 ms SDU interval.

detaileddescription.para: Once service discovery has completed and a stream is setup, it is recommended to switch to BT_BAP_CONN_PARAM_RELAXED
Defined by Table 8.3 in BAP 1.0.2

briefdescription.para: Recommended connection parameters for initial connection request for 10 ms SDU interval.

detaileddescription.para: Once service discovery has completed and a stream is setup, it is recommended to switch to BT_BAP_CONN_PARAM_RELAXED
Defined by Table 8.3 in BAP 1.0.2

briefdescription.para: Recommended connection parameters for coexistence of ACL and ISO.

detaileddescription.para: Defined by Table 8.3 in BAP 1.0.2

briefdescription.para: Fast advertising parameters for broadcast audio.

detaileddescription.para: This is suitable for both 7.5 ms and 10 ms SDU intervals, but prioritizes lower time to synchronize over coexistence with ISO and power consumption.

briefdescription.para: Slow advertising parameters for broadcast audio.

detaileddescription.para: This is suitable for both 7.5 ms and 10 ms SDU intervals, but prioritizes coexistence with ISO and power consumption over lower time to synchronize.

briefdescription.para: Fast advertising parameters for broadcast audio.

detaileddescription.para: This is suitable for both 7.5 ms and 10 ms SDU intervals, but prioritizes lower time to synchronize over coexistence with ISO and power consumption.

briefdescription.para: Slow advertising parameters for broadcast audio.

detaileddescription.para: This is suitable for both 7.5 ms and 10 ms SDU intervals, but prioritizes coexistence with ISO and power consumption over lower time to synchronize.

briefdescription.para: Check if a BAP BASS BIS_Sync bitfield is valid.

detaileddescription.para: Valid options are either a bitmask of valid BIS indices, including none (0x00000000) or BT_BAP_BIS_SYNC_NO_PREF (0xFFFFFFFF).

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare elements of bt_bap_qos_cfg.
Helper to declare Input Unframed bt_bap_qos_cfg.
Helper to declare Input Framed bt_bap_qos_cfg.
Helper to declare elements of bt_bap_qos_cfg_pref.
Value indicating that the periodic advertising interval is unknown.
Broadcast Assistant no BIS sync preference.

detaileddescription.para: Value indicating that the Broadcast Assistant has no preference to which BIS the Scan Delegator syncs to

briefdescription.para: BIS sync value indicating that the BIG sync has failed for any reason.
Macro used to initialise the object storing values of ASE Control Point notification.

parameterdescription.para: Reason field - bt_bap_ascs_reason or bt_audio_metadata_type (see notes in bt_bap_ascs_rsp).

briefdescription.para: Bluetooth Basic Audio Profile (BAP)

detaileddescription.para:
3.0
0.8.0
The Basic Audio Profile (BAP) allows for both unicast and broadcast Audio Stream control.
